author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
apāra karuṇā⋅sindhu vaiṣṇava ṭhākura
mo hena pāmare dayā karaha pracura

jāti⋅vidyā⋅dhana⋅jana⋅made matta jane
uddhāra kara he nātha, kṛpāvitaraṇe

kanaka⋅kāminī⋅lobha, pratiṣṭhā⋅vāsanā
chāḍāiyā śodha more, e mora prārthanā

nāme ruci, jīve dayā, vaiṣṇave ullāsa
dayā kari’ deha more, ohe kṛṣṇa⋅dāsa

tomāra caraṇachāyā ekamātra āśā
jīvane maraṇe mātra āmāra bharasā
en_translation:
O Vaiṣṇava Master! You are a boundless ocean of mercy. Please bestow abundant mercy upon a wretch like me.

Please deliver those who are intoxicated by pride in their caste, education, wealth, and followers by distributing your mercy.

Greed for wealth, attraction to the opposite sex, and the desire for fame—please free me from these and purify me; this is my sincere prayer.

O servant of Lord Kṛṣṇa! Please mercifully bestow upon me a taste for the Holy Name, compassion for all living entities, and joy in the company of the devotees.

The shade of your lotus feet is my only hope. In life and in death, it is my only reliance.
